judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 hits all four expectations with explicit, actionable recommendations (especially for the staging-baked frontend bundle and the missing-migration-verification points), while Report 2 misses (b) and (c) by only diagnosing symptoms without stating the required fix/conclusion.

report_1.evidence: **`ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`** ... this key should be **omitted entirely** from prod... **`AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`**... Should be **removed entirely**... **`PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS`**... must be **removed entirely**... Both keys should be **fully absent** from prod config... **`LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN`**... Should **not be recreated** in prod; remove it

report_2.evidence: **Line 2 — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`.**... Change to `"false"`... **Line 3 — `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`.**... Change to `"false"`... **Line 4 — `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`.**... Remove it (set to `""`)

report_1.evidence: the shipped prod bundle would have `https://staging-api.example.com` baked in, and because the pipeline promotes the same digest, there is no runtime fix. This is the "rebuild per environment / don't promote a baked artifact" trap exactly. Prod needs its own `.env.production` (e.g. `https://api.example.com`) and a separate `vite build --mode production`
confirm whether the first prod deploy actually runs migrations or needs a manual first pass — do not assume "pipeline succeeded" = schema current.

the prod role's condition must be changed to `...:environment:production`, and prod should get its **own scoped role** (both envs currently declare the same role name `medislot-deploy`), never an inherited/shared staging condition

report_2.evidence: That will **not** match the `staging` condition, so prod deploy authentication will fail. It must be changed to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`.
